entry.title: Low-Code and No-Code Development with SAP BTP: Empowering Business Users
entry.content:
Low-Code and No-Code Development with SAP BTP: Empowering Business Users

In today's fast-moving digital world, businesses need to innovate quickly. But let’s be honest—traditional software development can be slow, expensive, and requires highly skilled developers. This makes it hard for non-technical users to actively contribute to building solutions. This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) comes in, offering low-code and no-code (LCNC) development tools that allow business users to create applications with little to no coding experience.

What is Low-Code/No-Code Development?

Low-code and no-code platforms simplify application development by using visual, drag-and-drop interfaces instead of complex coding. These platforms help teams prototype quickly, bring ideas to life, and empower business users—sometimes called citizen developers—to take charge of their digital transformation.

How SAP BTP Makes Low-Code/No-Code Development Possible

SAP BTP offers several tools to make development easier for both technical and non-technical users:

1. SAP AppGyver – No-Code App Development

SAP AppGyver is a powerful no-code platform that lets users create web and mobile apps without writing any code. Here’s what makes it stand out:

  • Drag-and-drop UI builder for intuitive app design.

  • Easy integration with SAP and external systems.

  • Pre-built logic components to streamline workflows.

  • Cross-platform deployment, meaning apps work on different devices.

2. SAP Business Application Studio – Low-Code Development

For users who need more flexibility, SAP Business Application Studio provides a low-code development environment, offering:

  • Ready-made templates for SAP Fiori applications.

  • Seamless connection to SAP S/4HANA.

  • Extensibility with JavaScript and SAP UI5.

  • Cloud-native development with multi-environment support.

3. SAP Process Automation – Automate Workflows

SAP Process Automation combines workflow automation and robotic process automation (RPA) to:

  • Eliminate repetitive manual tasks.

  • Digitize approval workflows.

  • Boost productivity with automated processes.

Real-World Use Cases of SAP BTP LCNC Tools

Businesses across different industries are using SAP BTP’s LCNC capabilities to speed up digital transformation. Here are some practical applications:

  • Retail: Creating custom order-tracking apps without IT involvement.

  • Finance: Automating invoice approvals and reducing errors.

  • HR: Developing internal employee self-service portals.

  • Manufacturing: Building predictive maintenance dashboards for machinery.

Why Use Low-Code/No-Code with SAP BTP?

  • Speed & Agility: Faster development cycles mean quicker results.

  • Cost Savings: Reduces reliance on IT and expensive developers.

  • Empower Business Users: Enables domain experts to create solutions tailored to their needs.

  • Seamless SAP Integration: Works effortlessly with SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and other SAP applications.

Final Thoughts

SAP BTP’s low-code and no-code tools make application development accessible to everyone—not just developers. With these tools, businesses can innovate faster, automate processes, and drive digital transformation without the traditional barriers of coding.

Have you tried building an app using SAP BTP’s low-code/no-code tools? Share your experiences in the comments—we’d love to hear how these solutions are making a difference in your organization!

entry.title: Shape the Future of the Next-Generation Inbox
entry.content:
Attention My Inbox Users! 

Welcome to the future of task management with SAP Task Center, the next-generation inbox designed to streamline your workflow and keep you organized. If you use My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your input is vital for making SAP Task Center truly exceptional. 

What is SAP Task Center? 

SAP Task Center is a unified inbox for task management across multiple SAP and non-SAP solutions. It aims to provide a centralized hub where you can manage all your tasks efficiently, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks. 

Why Your Feedback Matters 

As a user of My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your feedback is crucial for our development process. By sharing your experiences - the good, the bad, and everything in between - you can directly shape the evolution of SAP Task Center. This collaborative effort ensures that the next-generation inbox is tailored to your specific needs and workflows.

How You Can Help

Participate in our survey and let us know what you think about My Inbox. Your insights will help us build a tool that truly understands and meets your workflow requirements.

Survey: Help Shape the Future of SAP Task Center

Together, we can make SAP Task Center an inbox that not only meets but exceeds your expectations. Your time and contributions are greatly appreciated.

If you’re curious to learn more about SAP Task Center, check out the SAP Task Center Product Page.

Thank you for being a part of this journey. Let’s build something remarkable together!

entry.title: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • An overview of guided experiences
  • Updated Features and Content
    • Restarting Guided Experiences
    • Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences
    • Collapsible Sections
    • Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Conclusion
  • Additional Read

An overview of guided experiences

In our last blog, we elaborated how Guided Experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Powered by SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation, Guided Experiences can help navigate through complex business processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hire or supplier onboarding, training and enablement, or browsing and & purchasing electronic equipment. Users can model intuitive stages and stages which can by further enriched with content, documents, forms, UI cards and more. Please see our previous blog to learn more about modelling a Guided Experience directly in SAP Build Process Automation from a Guided Process Artefact, creating stages and steps within each stage , deploying to Work Zone and connecting Guided Process as Wizard template in SAP Build Work Zone. In this blog, we will elaborate on the recently released advanced capabilities like multi-instance support, restarting a guided experience, having collapsible sections and pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors.

Updated Features and Content

Restarting Guided Experiences

One of the key updates is the ability to restart guided experiences. This feature allows users to reset and begin the experience from the start, providing flexibility and ensuring that users can correct any mistakes made during the initial run. The restart feature is also very helpful for easily demoing guided experiences. It allows presenters to quickly reset the process and demonstrate the guided experience multiple times without having to manually undo each step.
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Restart Button
The restart functionality is implemented as a button within the guided experience cover. When users click this button, they are prompted with a warning message that informs them that all progress will be lost, and the guided experience will start over from the beginning. 

Restart Guided Experience

A warning message is provided as a confirmation step, preventing accidental restarts / data loss and ensuring that users make an informed decision:
Warning message when restarting guided experience

Enable/Disable Setting
To provide further customization, the restart feature can be enabled or disabled per wizard. This setting allows administrators to control whether the restart option is available for specific guided experiences. Depending on the use case, restarting may not be applicable or feasible, in which cases this option can be disabled in the wizard settings.
Setting to enable restart

Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences

Another significant enhancement is the introduction of multi-instance support. This feature enables multiple instances of a guided experience to run simultaneously, allowing users to manage and track various processes concurrently. This is very useful for scenarios where multiple tasks need to be handled in parallel.Multi-instance overview for guided experience
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Use Cases
Multi-instance support can be applied to various guided experience use cases, including but not limited to:

  • Event Planning - Organizing multiple events simultaneously, each with its own set of tasks and requirements.
  • Purchasing IT Equipment - Managing multiple purchase requests for IT equipment, ensuring that each request is tracked and processed independently.
  • Grievances Requests - Handling multiple grievance requests concurrently, allowing for efficient resolution and tracking of each request.

Managing and Viewing All Instances
To facilitate the management of multiple instances, users can view all active instances in a dedicated interface. This interface provides options to:

  • Add Name - Assign a unique name to each instance for easy identification.
  • Restart Instance - Reset an instance to its initial state, allowing users to start the process over.
  • Cancel Instance - Terminate a given guided experience instance, removing it from the active list.

Multi-instance detail view for guided experience

Enable / Disable Setting
Similar to the restart feature, multi-instance support can be enabled or disabled per guided experience. This setting allows workpage owners to control whether the multi-instance option is available for the specific guided experience. Depending on the use case, running multiple instances may not be applicable or feasible, and this option can be disabled in the wizard settings in such a scenario.

Setting to enable multi-instance

Collapsible Sections

The collapsible sections feature is designed to improve the user interface for guided experiences and freestyle sections alike, providing flexibility for both enduser & content creators working with workpages.


Section Settings
Each workpage section comes with dedicated settings, including the option to make it collapsible. This allows users to personalize different sections based on their preferences and needs. Sections can be set to either expanded or collapsed by default, providing a tailored experience for each scenario. A typical use case is creating an FAQ page with many sections where the collapsed by default structure the workpage content nicely.


Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Freestyle Layout
For freestyle layout sections, it is recommended to include a title or information for the dropdown. The title and information act as a header, making it clear what each section contains when collapsed.

Wizard Layout
In guided experiences, collapsible sections support various scenarios such as starting an instance, continuing an instance, and more. These collapsed sections also include a progress bar to visually indicate the user's progress of the guided experience. This feature is particularly useful for placing guided experiences space-efficiently on workpages without cluttering the interface. Example guided experiences in collapsed view:

Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors

We are excited to introduce pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors, designed to streamline HR processes and enhance user experience. This content includes ready-to-use templates for employee onboarding and cross-boarding content packages, which can be easily deployed in SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation.

Content Availability
The pre-built content for Process Automation is available in the SAP Build Lobby store, while the content for Work Zone is provided as dedicated content packages via the Work Zone admin UI. These packages - among many other items - also feature a re-usable, configurable UI Card for item lists, allowing for a more personalized user experience for both users and content creators alike.

Scenarios of Content
There are three scenarios of pre-built content available:

  • Legacy (UI5 component card + workflow) - This scenario includes a single UI5 component card per guided experience combined with a workflow. It is however not recommended for new setups due to its outdated structure, limited flexibility & complex modification approach.
  • Work Zone Wizard - This scenario involves a Work Zone local wizard template, including UI cards. It provides a streamlined and intuitive experience for users, allowing them to navigate through processes with ease.
  • Process Automation version - This scenario is the combined version of Work Zone and Process Automation content. It leverages the strengths of both platforms to provide a comprehensive solution for managing complex processes.

For more detailed instructions on how to leverage this pre-built content, please refer to the following SAP Help guide. In this guide we also provide a detailed comparison table of those three scenarios of guided experience pre-built content.

 

Conclusion

The guided experiences are changing the way businesses can manage complex processes. From creating end-to-end onboarding experience for new employees to the application of parental leave, guided experiences are leveraging various capabilities within SAP Build as a deeply integrated, unified solution experience.

 

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone: How to Create a Wizard
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard
  • Video example Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences
  • 2024 Devtoberfest Guided Experience recording: https://www.youtube.com/live/q1pmO283Y5c?si=wfNm8eDNJPC0t_k8

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Build continues to evolve to support how modern teams work — now with more artificial intelligence, flexibility, and speed. In Q1 2025, we delivered a wave of innovations across SAP Build Code, Process Automation, Apps, and Work Zone to help developers and business users turn ideas into enterprise-ready solutions faster than ever.

Whether you're building custom apps, creating SAP S/4HANA extensions, or streamlining processes, these updates will help you move from concept to value more efficiently.  Let's review the top 10 updates we selected from last quarter.

Licensing and Availability Updates

1. SAP Task Center Now included with SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Task Center is now embedded in all SAP Build Work Zone editions, giving users a unified view of workflow tasks from multiple SAP systems — right within their Work Zone homepage. No separate integration is required. This update also extends to partner licensing, making including Task Center in custom Work Zone scenarios easier. The result is faster approvals, centralized task tracking, and higher productivity for business users and managers — all in one workspace.

R

2. SAP Build Apps Now Available in More Regions

You can now deploy SAP Build Apps in EU11 and Sydney data centers, bringing your applications to more users. The increased coverage improves app responsiveness, supports region-specific compliance needs, and enables global scalability for your enterprise use cases.

Joule for Developers Updates

3. Generate End-to-End Workflows with a Prompt

In SAP Build Process Automation, developers can now describe business scenarios in natural language — and let generative AI create forms, decisions, and process flows automatically. Powered by SAP's purpose-built large language models (LLMs), this capability delivers contextualized, SAP-aware outcomes that speed up workflow creation and reduce manual effort. It also empowers business users to contribute to process design without writing code.

4. Generate UI Pages from Data in SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps now uses generative AI to propose user interface pages based on your data model. For example, if your app contains entities like Books, Authors, or Genres, Joule recommends corresponding screens, layouts, and controls. This helps teams move quickly from data modeling to interactive UI design, shortening prototyping cycles and improving design consistency.

5. Use Generative AI for Fiori UI Creation in SAP Build Code

Developers can now use Joule in SAP Build Code to generate Fiori-based UIs for existing CAP apps by entering the command /add-ui or selecting the Joule icon in the Storyboard. Joule understands your application structure and proposes relevant layouts, controls, and navigation patterns, helping you scaffold front-end apps quickly and focus on customization instead of starting from scratch.

6. Multi-Agent Support for CAP App Generation

SAP Build Code now uses multi-agent support powered by Joule to generate complete CAP applications. Each agent focuses on a specific part — like data models, services, or UI — so you get a more complete project from a single prompt. This enhancement saves time and helps you focus on business logic instead of building everything from scratch.

Read also Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code by david_bizer.

Enhance SAP Business Suite Applications

7. Extend SAP S/4HANA with Custom UIs (Planned for Release Soon)

Coming very soon in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, developers will be able to use the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard to create side-by-side extensions—either by customizing existing SAP Fiori apps or building entirely new web applications. This approach enables teams to meet specific business needs while preserving the core system, supporting clean core principles and long-term agility.

  • See documentation: Using the Extensibility Wizard.
  • On setup, read the blog SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by @Karthik-Thiru. 

8. New Enhancements for Guided Experiences by SAP Build

New enhancements to Guided Experiences by SAP Build help simplify complex processes like employee onboarding, parental leave, procurement approvals, and IT service management, where multiple steps, systems, and stakeholders are involved. With this release, ready-to-use content packages for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding and Cross-boarding are now available in the SAP Build Store—enabling faster rollout of consistent, step-by-step guidance for new hires and internal transfers.

  • Read more: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level by florian_buech.
  • Watch the 2-minute video: Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences.

9. Create a Wait for an API Call Trigger

With new support for intermediate message events, SAP Build Process Automation lets workflows pause until specific API-triggered inputs are received and resume automatically. This powerful capability reduces wait times and manual monitoring, helping teams design more adaptive and responsive processes with fewer errors and overhead. 

See documentation: Create a Wait for an API Call Trigger | SAP Help Portal

Boost Developer Productivity

10. Revamped Project Overview in SAP Build Code

A redesigned Project Overview in SAP Build Code provides a clean, tree-based view of all app components, supporting CAP Java and CAP Node. This enhanced layout integrates with the Storyboard, making it easier for developers to navigate complex projects, onboard new team members, and stay focused while building.

See documentation: Project Overview | SAP Help Portal

Ready to See These Updates in Action?

These are just a few of the top updates rolling out this quarter. Want to explore the new features in more detail, see live demos, and get your questions answered by product experts?

👉Join us on April 15 for the What's New in SAP Build Q1 2025 webinar.
Reserve your spot to discover these updates in action. Register below:

📅There are two options to cover different time zones.
🕘9:00 – 10:00 AM CET → https://sap.to/604200bfg
🕔5:00 – 6:00 PM CET → https://sap.to/604500bfc

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • SAP Build Help Portal
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

Please stay current and connect with fellow SAP Builders by joining our Application Development and Automation Community for the latest updates, activities, and challenges.

entry.title: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
The 👉 SAP Community Escape House has a new addition!!

We've built a garage for our workshop to build all kinds of stuff using SAP Build. Use your wits and knowledge about SAP Build – specifically SAP Build Process Automation – to try to escape.

WARNING: It will be really tough to escape – you have been warned! So I will be providing tips throughout the month to help you through, so subscribe to this post.

For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges created by the Developer Advocates will happen in the SAP Community Escape House (shout out to @nicoschoenteich for his amazing creativity). Each month we will unlock a new room in the house with a specific focus. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding – or no-code 😉 – tasks/questions. This month is all about one of the tools inside SAP Build.

Here are our previous challenges:

  • January: UI5
  • February: Integration Suite
  • March: AI

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID.

If you don't know your user ID, click Need Help? This will display a link to your community settings page.

Then you will see your ID here:

Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the SAP Build garage.

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips.

Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room.

The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community – and the unending esteem of your fellow SAP developers, business users and implementers. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

Good luck!

😺

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 3rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/03/sap-developer-news-april-3rd-2025/ 

Developer Challenge 

  • Blog: TK 
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/house 

 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation  

  • Blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-brings-generative-ai-to-process-automation-to-enable-your/ba-p/14049869  
  • SAP Help Portal : https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/generate-process?locale=en-US  

 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/create-sap-build-apps-applications-in-minutes-with-ai/ba-p/14054862 
  • SAP Help portal https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/generate-pages-using-ai 

 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

  • Registration form: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=bGf3QlX0PEKC9twtmXka9zoyeoeObGdNh1zA5HmpzP5UMEVIN0xWUlBRWUlCWUlQUVJKV1pZMDFBWS4u  

  

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Developer Challenge 

1:12 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation 

2:08 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

3:08 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for April 3rd, 2025.

[Daniel] You might have heard the sound of heavy machinery working on an addition to the SAP community escape house. Well, I'm happy to say that the SAP build garage is now ready. April's Developer Challenge will deal with a specific and key feature of SAP build. Well, I can tell you that. You have to discover that for yourself. It will include spy gear, exercise equipment, something from last month's AI challenge, and a few things that you won't see when you first enter the room. It will be a real challenge, no doubt about it. But if you're able to get out of the garage alive, please add a comment to my blog post and tell me how it went. Good luck.

[Rekha DR] Hello everybody! Are you a developer using SAP Build tools to build applications and automations? Then here is a good news for you to amplify productivity and efficiency in your day-to-day work. You can now use Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation. You can enable Generative AI for your BTP tenant within the control tower of SAP. and then you can use Generative AI to generate a business process, a data type or a form from the overview screen of the project. Within the process, you can create an automation task, explain the business process in natural language. Generate new code in the script task, explain a decision or generate business rules. For more details, check this blog post by product expert, Yogananda Muthaiah provided in the description box. Keep learning, keep exploring. Thank you.  

[Shrinivasan] SAP applications are continuing to evolve with generative AI capabilities, and these capabilities are now available in SAP Build Apps. There is a new AI powered feature available in our no-code development environment now. SAP Build Apps now includes a generative AI feature that makes it easier to create pages for create, read, update operations on you. your existing OData backup. Simply enable an identity from the Integrations tab, and you will see the new Generate Pages option. Keep in mind, you will need to enable the Generative AI feature from the Control Tower first to use this option. Once generated, these pages can be customized and extended further to suit your specific needs. Note that the generated Pages feature comes with a usage limit of 100 requests per month for each user. Explore this new functionality to to jumpstart your application development. The links to the blog post and the help portal on this new feature is available in the description.

[Antonio] Holla, SAP Database Administrators and Database Developers. Have you ever wanted to provide feedback on the SAP HANA Cloud database service? Well, now you can. There will be an SAP BTP usability event on the 13th and 14th of May in Walldorf Germany. This is an in-person event where you can gain insights on the SAP's product vision. Also, you can share ideas directly with the development teams and provide your input on new features directly to the engineering teams. If you're interested in participating, make sure to register on the link that I'm going to share in the description of this video. Thank you very much for your time.Ciao

entry.title: New documentation available: Extend your SAP S/4HANA Processes with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
"Intelligent Processes Are Integrated Processes"

If you're already automating and extending business processes on the SAP Business Technology Platform, you're likely thinking about the best way to connect your new process extensions to the relevant systems of record. This also includes SAP Build Process Automation, which serves as our central development environment for creating intelligent automations and orchestrating end-to-end business processes across complex IT landscapes.

The good news is that there is now a comprehensive new guide available on the SAP Help Portal to help with this process:

  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Processes with SAP Build Process Automation

This guide walks you through the integration setup and management of SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It is primarily aimed at system administrators and technical consultants who support customers using SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud and wish to extend their ERP capabilities by integrating with SAP Build Process Automation.

Covering the most crucial activities for successful integration, this guide is your go-to resource for connecting SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

But first of all what is SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation is a solution designed for citizen developers to adapt, enhance, and innovate business processes through no-code workflow management and robotic process automation.

It empowers both business users and technologists to take on the role of citizen developers. With its powerful, yet user-friendly low-code and no-code features, SAP Build Process Automation allows you to drive automation by leveraging the expertise of your citizen developer community. It allows you to:

  • Digitalize, extend, or adapt existing processes and orchestrate new end-to-end processes
  • Seamlessly leverage UiPath capabilities with SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath
  • Build RPA bots to handle repetitive tasks so users can focus on higher-value goals
  • Accelerate document-heavy processes with intelligent document processing
  • Connect process and task automations to any SAP or non-SAP application

 

 What can you learn in this new documentation?

You should certainly bookmark this documentation to learn how to integrate your custom processes and automations with SAP S/4HANA, and extend standardized workflows to meet specific business requirements.

The runtime setup involves several configurations that must be executed in both the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit and the SAP Build Lobby. In this guide, we’ll walk through a common use case: pushing and retrieving business partner master data into an SAP S/4HANA system, whether on-premise or in the cloud.

Below, you can review the final architectures that you can achieve once you’ve completed the documentation. First, here’s the technical architecture for integrating SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA on-premise:

 

Next, here’s the technical architecture for integrating SAP Build Process Automation with SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

 

 

Conclusion

It’s important to note that this documentation focuses on the integration between SAP Build Process Automation and SAP S/4HANA. However, the same approach can be applied to integrate with other SAP applications, such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, or SAP Customer Experience, as well as third-party systems. The setup can also be reused for processing various types of transactional or master data within a system of record. I hope this new asset will help you to simplify and standardize the connectivity o custom processes and automations to your existing backend applications.

entry.title: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
📢  Today is my last day working for SAP.

Indeed, I've been accepted into the very last round of the Voluntary Early Retirement Plan launched by SAP France in 2024, for which I applied the day of my 62nd birthday, on March 10.

It has been quick, but I'm happy. I thanks my management hierarchy Diana Roesner / André Borchert / Thomas Volmering / Steffen Pietsch / Michael Ameling / Muhammad Alam / Christian Klein (*) for giving me the unique opportunity to stop working 33 months earlier than expected! 😊

(*) yes, I was very low in SAP org chart 😉 but I do not care: what matters to me is to bring my expertise where and when it makes a real impact, and I humbly think it was the case during my 6.5-year tenure in this great company. 

I will really miss my wonderful team of such lovely colleagues in charge of SAP Build Product Management, Enablement and Communications 🥹  ... but I will still be hanging out here on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to follow what they will be doing with SAP Build and broadly SAP Business Technology Platform with Artificial Intelligence and Joule, because I know what will be announced in a few weeks at SAP Sapphire 2025, and for sure they will rock the stage!

 

I would like to heartfully thank Diana Roesner, André Borchert, Thomas Volmering, Michael Ameling, Sebastian Schrötel, Bhagat Nainani, Juergen Mueller and all the colleagues for having supporting me during my 6+ years at SAP, during this amazing journey from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build. 🙏🏻

👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓 I seize this opportunity to promote 4️⃣ learnings on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Build.

Indeed, I won't spoil anything but there will be tremendous announcements in a few weeks: leverage these learnings to be prepared!

✅ An Enterprise Architect’s View on SAP Business Technology Platform

✅ Experiencing End-To-End SAP Build

✅ Compose and Automate with SAP Build the No-Code Way

✅ Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

📖  Let me also recommend the SAP Build Process Automation book from SAP PRESS!

Order this book now and learn how to create processes, automations, actions, and decisions by following step-by-step instructions with this expert-written guide by my dear colleagues @ArchanaShukla, @Chaitanya_Priya Puvvada, @Stephan Schluchter, Seshadri Sreenivas, and Steven Jacobson.

Finally, I would like to say that I'm very happy because I will spend 2 days in Paris April 14-15, 2 days in Walldorf May 12-13 and finally 2 days in Berlin May 14-15 to hang out with my colleagues and say a warm and grateful goodbye: if you will be there, come say hi! 🥲

And now, for me it's time to…

 

 💙 I wish you all the very best! 💙

entry.title: SAP Build 新功能：2025年一季度版本亮点
entry.content:
本文本为翻译文，原文档链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights，原作者 Jesse Sukhbayar。

SAP Build 不断发展，利用更多人工智能、灵活性和速度，为现代团队的工作方式提供支持。2025 年第 1 季度，我们针对 SAP Build Code、Process Automation、Apps 和 Work Zone 推出了一系列创新成果，帮助开发人员和业务用户比以往更快地将创意转化为企业级解决方案。

无论您是构建自定义应用、创建 SAP S/4HANA 扩展还是简化流程，这些更新都将帮助您更高效地从概念过渡到价值。  我们来回顾一下从上一季度中选择的前 10 项更新。

 

许可和可用性更新

1.SAP Task Center 现在包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 中

SAP Task Center 现在嵌入在所有 SAP Build Work Zone 版本中，为用户提供来自多个 SAP 系统的工作流任务的统一视图 - 位于其 Work Zone 主页中。无需单独集成。此更新还扩展到合作伙伴许可，使自定义 Work Zone 场景中的 Task Center 更容易。这样，业务用户和管理人员就可以在一个工作区中加快审批速度，集中跟踪任务，并提高工作效率。

  1. SAP Build Apps 现在可在更多区域使用

现在，您可以在 EU11 和悉尼数据中心部署 SAP Build Apps，将应用程序提供给更多用户。增加的覆盖范围可提高应用的响应能力，支持区域特定的合规性需求，并为企业用例提供全局可扩展性。

 

Joule for Developers 

3.使用提示生成端到端工作流

在 SAP Build Process Automation 中，开发人员现在可以使用自然语言描述业务场景，并允许生成性 AI 自动创建表单、决策和流程流。此功能由 SAP 专门构建的大型语言模型 (LLM) 提供支持，可提供基于情境的 SAP 感知结果，从而加快工作流创建速度并减少手动工作量。它还使业务用户能够在无需编写代码的情况下对流程设计做出贡献。

4.从 SAP Build Apps 中的数据生成 UI 页面

SAP Build Apps 现在使用生成式 AI 根据您的数据模型建议用户界面页面。例如，如果您的应用包含 Books、Authors 或 Genres 等实体，Joule 会推荐相应的屏幕、布局和控件。这有助于团队快速从数据建模转变为交互式 UI 设计，缩短原型设计周期并提高设计一致性。

5.在 SAP Build Code 中使用 Generative AI 创建 Fiori UI

开发人员现在可以通过输入命令 /add-ui 或在故事板中选择焦耳图标，在 SAP 构建代码中使用 Joule 为现有 CAP 应用生成基于 Fiori 的 UI。焦耳了解您的应用程序结构，并建议相关的布局、控件和导航模式，帮助您快速使用脚手架前端应用，并专注于定制，而不是从头开始。

6.CAP 应用生成的多代理支持

SAP Build Code 现在使用由 Joule 支持的多代理支持来生成完整的 CAP 应用程序。每个代理都专注于特定部分（如数据模型、服务或 UI），因此您可以从单个提示中获取更完整的项目。此增强节省了时间，可帮助您关注业务逻辑，而不是从头开始构建所有内容。

另请阅读 SAP Build Code 中的所有 GenAI 选项概览 ， david_bizer .

 

增强 SAP Business Suite 应用程序

7.使用自定义 UI 扩展 SAP S/4HANA（计划即将发布）

即将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）中推出，开发人员将能够使用 SAP Build 可扩展性向导创建并排扩展 - 通过定制现有 SAP Fiori 应用或构建全新的 Web 应用程序。这种方法使团队能够满足特定的业务需求，同时保留核心系统，支持精简核心原则和长期灵活性。

  • 请参阅文档：使用可扩展性向导。
  • 在设置中，请阅读博客 @Karthik-Thiru 提供的 SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公共版。 

8.SAP Build 引导式体验的新增强功能

SAP Build 对引导式体验进行了新的增强，有助于简化复杂的流程，如员工入职、育儿假、采购审批和 IT 服务管理，其中涉及多个步骤、系统和利益相关方。 在此版本中，现在可以在 SAP Build Store 中获取 SAP SuccessFactors Employee Onboarding 和转岗的即用型内容包，从而更快地为新员工和内部调动提供一致的逐步指导。

  • 开始： SAP Build 引导式体验 ，florian_buech
  • 观看视频（2 分钟）：借助 SAP Build 引导式体验，规划育儿假。

9.实时工作流的中间消息事件

通过对中间消息事件的新支持，SAP Build Process Automation 允许工作流暂停，直到自动接收并恢复特定 API触发的输入为止。这种强大的功能可减少等待时间和手动监控，帮助团队设计更具适应性和响应能力的流程，同时减少错误和开销。 

 

提高开发人员工作效率

10.SAP Build Code 中改进的项目概览

SAP Build Code 中重新设计的项目概览提供了所有应用组件的基于树的清洁视图，支持 CAP Java 和 CAP 节点。此增强的布局与故事板集成，使开发人员能够更轻松地导航复杂项目、启用新团队成员并在构建时保持专注度。

了解更多

有关新功能的完整列表，请查看以下资源：

  • SAP Build 帮助门户
  • SAP Build Road Map Explorer

entry.title: SAP Ariba SLP - Optical character recognition and Extraction for Supplier Registration Process
entry.content:
Customer Problem Definition
Currently, the supplier registration process supports the upload documents, such as PDF, Word, etc., which are kept in the different questionnaires as attachments which must then be processed manually to obtain the required information, thus opening the possibility of introducing errors, increasing the supplier registration time and requiring a person to manually review the content to approve it or ask the supplier to correct the information.

Customer Value Proposition
Have an automated workflow that processes the information in attached documents to:

  1. Validate that the information is as required; if not, automatically request the supplier to correct it.
  2. Extract information from documents to enrich it and update internal forms.

In a previous article, I mentioned how to extend this same process but with AI capabilities. Take a look if you missed it  Extending SLP functionalities with SAP BTP and Art... - SAP Community

Solution Architecture Design

On the Ariba side, we will extract and write the information via APIs. This extraction will be done via SAP Build Process Automation, which is a BTP service and already has OCR for our purposes.

Solution Business Process Design

As you can see in the proposed design, the starting point is based on the tasks generated by the supplier registration flow, which gives us the ability to efficiently process new requests and questionnaire updates. 

SAP Ariba Supplier Registration Process Design

On the other hand, once the supplier has completed the required information, the automation can be responsible for validating that the information provided is correct. If it is not, it can request its correction. If all the information is correct, it can auto-approve the process and/or leave it in a final state where a person can make the final approval decision.

SAP Build Process Automation Document Information Extraction Process Design

In the scenario shown, we worked on the premise that the supplier must upload a legal document in this country, which contains standard fields for extraction.

At SAP Build Process Automation there are two options to perform the OCR with different capabilities each one

Results & Conclusions 

The following is an example of the standard legal document uploaded to the supplier registration questionnaire, extracted via API by BTP, processed by applying OCR and writing the desired fields in an internal questionnaire of the supplier registration project in SAP Ariba.

Disclaimer: Test Legal document - No valid information

On the SAP Ariba side, we can validate that the approval nodes are auto-rejected to request information correction and/or auto-approved if the information provided is correct.

More Information About:

APIs

Blog - Consume Ariba APIs using Postman

SAP Ariba Developer Portal
SAP Ariba API Documentation

SAP Build Process Automation

Discovery Center

entry.title: Deadline Monitoring in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Problem Statement

  • In traditional SAP workflow deadline monitoring comes by standard on dialog task allowing SLA driven notification and approval to be managed easily.
  • This is also one of most common requirements in SAP Build Process Automation.  In SAP Build Process Automation there is no default deadline monitoring where workitem goes into obsolete and obsolete branch can be modeled for further level of processing.
  • This means if it is not incorporated then the threre can be SLA breaches or lags in process and better time to market.

Solution

The solution for escalation in SAP Build Process Automation is Timer events. You can have multiple timer events, such as sending an email after 5 days and sending a workflow approval after 10 days. Check the screenshot below:

*Image1*

We will also be using a Timer event based on “After Task creation,” and after this event, we will be using “Skip to next step” as shown below: 

*Image2*

We will also insert a subprocess that takes the same input as the Input Form. The subprocess output is a flag mapped to a custom variable in the main process, indicating whether the Escalation Manager has “Approved” or “Rejected” the task. See the screenshots of the subprocess and output mapping below:

*Image 3*

*Image 4*        

As we selected "skip to next step" in Image 2, it will skip to the 1st Branch (Approval branch). Here again, we need to check if the work item was approved or rejected by using the custom variable we mapped from the output of the subprocess. We also need to initialize this as true by default since we are in the approval branch. In case the custom variable flag is false, i.e., the escalation manager rejected it, we will branch it to reject branch of main workflow. The final workflow will look something like below.

*Image5*

Conclusion

Using the above approach ensures proper deadline monitoring with multiple levels of approvals and ensures timely completion of processes with no SLA breaches.

I hope you found this blog post informative and helpful. Please share your feedback, it would be helpful to learn and explore more.

Thanks for taking your time & going through the blog.

entry.title: Unlocking the Potential of SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath
entry.content:
The rise of artificial intelligence (AI) and robotic process automation (RPA) technologies have empowered companies to leave behind manual, error-prone, and time-consuming work for streamlined operations, customized processes, and end-to-end automated solutions. The most competitive enterprises today are developing a lasting edge by taking advantage of these capabilities.

To give our customers an even greater edge with their automation toolbox, in Q4 2024 we released SAP’s Solution Extension with UiPath to integrate with their leading RPA to automate non-SAP environments.

With the new SAP Build Process Automations integrated with UiPath, customers can extend the entire SAP enterprise automation framework, whether they be between SAP and non-SAP applications, on-premises and multi-cloud environments, and systems that span customers, employees and partners.

 

How UiPath extends the value of enterprise automation with SAP
Enterprise automation from SAP uses best-in-class SAP technologies – SAP Integration Suite, SAP Signavio solutions, AI Agents and now SAP Build integrated with UiPath – to help connect and automate complex, end-to-end business processes. These technologies work in concert to enable customers to quickly and easily connect any on-premises or cloud IT landscape, including SAP and non-SAP systems, to gain visibility into business processes and their performance, and then automate those processes seamlessly.

With this partnership, it provides SAP customers flexibility to maintain their UiPath investments while leveraging a single process orchestration layer for both SAP Build Process Automation and UiPath robots to automate non-SAP processes.  As for automating SAP processes, we continue to invest in SAP Build Process Automation and its extensive pre-build workflows and automations for SAP applications. It also serves as the foundation for Joule Studio in SAP Build that customers can use to build AI agents.

 

Fast track cloud migration with proven accelerators
The move from customized ERP systems to S/4HANA Cloud is time consuming and risky while ensuring data integrity during the complex migration process.  By leveraging SAP Activate methodology and SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath robots with proven migration content, data is accurately extracted by identifying complex code, analyze vulnerabilities, check for compatibility with S/4 and remediate any issues automatically to simplify migration efforts and prevents manual errors.

Continuous process improvement to surpass competition
Organizations face continuous changes in processes, applications and industry best practices, which can break automated strategic end-to-end business processes.  To maintain process transparency, enterprise automation with SAP portfolio includes integrated triggers that recognize inefficiencies or events, and to adjust the predetermined behavior of automation methodologies for greater productivity in S/4HANA.  To minimize disruption from application changes, SAP iPaaS and RPA can automate core processes and utilize automation techniques.  As for best practices to remain competitive, SAP is the process leader across 25 industries where knowledge is captured in industry proven benchmark and pre-built content delivered through SAP Business Accelerator hub that can be rapidly deployed.


Automate enterprise processes to optimize your business
Automating strategic end-to-end processes often require a variety of automation technologies (RPA, workflow, systems integration, APIs and events powered by AI) across heterogenous landscapes.  Enterprise automation with SAP portfolio is a Gartner market leader with profiling, iPaaS, and now RPA, which provides the process orchestration layer for comprehensive interoperability across all these automation methods.

SAP and UiPath Together: One Platform, Limitless Potential
By integrating SAP Build Process Automation workflows with UiPath’s RPA and third-party connectors, we’re offering our customers one of the largest libraries of pre-built content and accelerators to help address their comprehensive processes that span multiple applications within a complex IT landscape—regardless of the cloud software provider.  To illustrate the power of SAP and UiPath synergy, let’s examine a business scenario that shows real life automations across SAP and non-SAP environments as well as other highlights across the integrated solution.

Procurement: MRP-to-goods receipt journey
Manual interventions in the source-to-pay journey can weigh down business operations.  When processes traverse many different applications, tracking down the process exceptions or even the status of any process instance can be a laborious task requiring highly specialized skills.  Let’s take a look as to how SAP and UiPath can work together to deliver a seamless procurement journey.

The process starts with an MRP run in a manufacturing operations management solution, like Siemens Opcenter

  1. The creation of the purchase requisitions trigger the SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath to create these requisitions in SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  2. Once in Ariba, the created requisitions are routed to the correct approver based on the SAP Build Process Automation business rules engine
  3. Approved requisitions are converted to purchase orders in Ariba and Oracle WMS is updated with the PO data for goods receipt
  4. Finally, SAP S/4HANA Cloud is updated with all relevant data to ensure prompt and accurate invoice processing.

As you can see, this process is relatively complex, yet the power of SAP and UiPath enable seamless automation across the many systems as well as a central monitoring view to be able to quickly respond to any processing exceptions.

 

Process improvement
To gain a comprehensive view of your processes across your ERP landscape, you can pinpoint bottlenecks and uncover areas with the greatest automation potential. More importantly, with contextual best practices and prebuilt content for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other cloud solutions from SAP, your organization can receive actionable recommendations to refine its processes. For example, inefficiencies identified in SAP Signavio solutions can trigger smart actions in SAP Build—turning insights into impactful automations.

 

Enterprise-grade governance
Combine SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath’s capabilities and the comprehensive security features of SAP Business Technology Platform to help ensure compliance and trust in enterprise-wide automation efforts. 

 

Orchestrated task automation
Coordinate workflows with smooth connectivity to the SAP Task Center service, driving consistent and optimized operations.


Combined development tools
SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines workflow management, robotic process automation (RPA), and embedded AI into a unified low-code/no-code environment. Designed for business users and IT teams, the Automation designs originate as projects within SAP Build Process Automation, serving as the foundation for streamlined workflows and advanced process orchestration.

Key features include:

  • Integrated development tools to build business rules, approval workflows, RPA bots, and generative AI models
  • Comprehensive workflow management to orchestrate processes, monitor tasks in real time, and gain actionable insights.  Customizable process extensions help keep the ERP core clean while extending functionality with drag-and-drop tools and embedded security guardrails
  • Native integrations with SAP Integration Suite, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Signavio solutions, and other SAP software for a unified experience
  • Cost efficiency through native embedding
    Decrease integration and maintenance costs by embedding UiPath RPA directly into SAP Build Process Automation. Unlike standalone RPA solutions, SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath eliminate the need for extensive third-party integrations.
  • Advanced monitoring for real-time insights
    This integration features authentication enabled by SAP Cloud Identity Services that gives access to UiPath services, while lifecycle synchronization helps ensure automations remain aligned across both solutions. UiPath monitoring is also fully integrated into SAP Build Process Automation, providing unified tracking and enhanced visibility into automation processes.

 

  • Digital access license is not required to connect with SAP systems
  • Streamlined design and deployment
    Use drag-and-drop tools to efficiently build and deploy automation workflows. This capability simplifies the design process and accelerates automation implementation.  For example, workflows and processes can be streamlined by leverage bots directly within SAP Build Process Automation.

    •  

Advanced automation tools

  • Comprehensive automation at scale beyond SAP solutions: Launch automation projects with thousands of prebuilt templates for third-party systems to create a unified enterprise automation ecosystem across the business.
  • AI-driven automation for smarter workflows:  Leverage built-in AI capabilities, such as intelligent document processing and predictive analytics, to enhance the efficiency of automations. Unlike many standalone RPA solutions, the SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines AI and automation to handle complex, unstructured data and document processing into workflows.
  • Unified automation for complex processes:  Empower your business by automating entire workflows, helping ensure comprehensive process optimization across multiple systems and reducing fragmentation in automation efforts.
  • Cloud-based bot execution:  Transition to the cloud with bots to connect with SAP solutions securely and efficiently through remote calls and surface automation using screen recordings and UI-element captures.  This connectivity supports a range of SAP user interface technologies, including SAP Fiori apps, SAPUI5, GUI, WebGUI, and line-of-business software, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and third-party applications. With SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath, these options provide prebuilt content, native connectors, and superior surface automation for a seamless integration experience.
  • Process optimization with advanced insights:  Leverage task mining and live insights into SAP Signavio solutions, so you can uncover inefficiencies and transform processes with actionable data.

 

How UiPath strengthens Enterprise Automation with SAP
With more than 50 years of expertise in mission-critical business processes, SAP continues to lead the way in enterprise automation.  By combining trusted solutions with the latest technologies, SAP empowers organizations to simplify operations, drive innovation, and scale transformation.

SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath are part of that long-standing legacy. By offering a clear path to unify, optimize, and future-proof your processes, the integration not only elevate automation strategies but also unlock new levels of resilience and growth.

Ready to see how enterprise automation can transform your business? Interact with our demo of SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath.

 

To learn more, please check out our upcoming UiPath Solution Extension Blog Series from our SAP Product Engineering Owner, Deval Khatri, @devalk

entry.title: New Basic Trial available: Get your hands on Enterprise Automation with SAP
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business landscape, automation has emerged as a crucial competitive differentiator. While most stakeholders understand its strategic value and potential benefits, many organizations still face significant challenges on the path to becoming truly autonomous.

Enterprise Automation with SAP is the only solution that offers a comprehensive, integrated suite of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and continuous optimization of workflows. It empowers businesses to streamline operations and drive intelligent decision-making—without adding unnecessary complexity.

 

 

With SAP Signavio, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite, customers gain access to best-in-class capabilities. These tools work in harmony, enabling users to quickly uncover insights, automate processes, and integrate seamlessly with existing applications to deliver the right outcomes.

Now, there’s a powerful new way to explore the full potential of Enterprise Automation with SAP. The new Basic Trial gives you hands-on access to SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio—all in one end-to-end scenario. It’s the perfect opportunity to experience the value of automation in action. In order to activate your basic trial, you need to navigate to the Enterprise Automation webpage on SAP.com:

Access to Basic Trial for Enterprise Automation

As the next step you need to click on the ,,Start your free 30-day trial" button that will forward you to the next page.

 

 

You are now being forwarded to the Basic Trial page of SAP Build Process Automation, here you simply click on ,,Try now". This process will generate log-in credentials which you can use right away to start your hands-on tutorial.

 

 

After logging in you will be redirected to the tutorial landing page. As a last step you need to select the Enterprise Automation workbook and start your tutorial:

 

 

This includes a 2 hour hands-on exercise with 5 different units, which you can discover at your own pace:

 

 

If you want to learn more about the Basic Trial and the use case, that is being covered in this hands-on feel free to listen to our webcast, which you can find here.

 

Happy hacking!

entry.title: Introducing the New Project Creation Wizard within SAP Build Lobby
entry.content:
Introduction:

We are pleased to announce the launch of an enhanced Project Creation Wizard in the SAP Build Lobby, designed to accelerate and streamline your project initiation processes. This new tool reflects our commitment to incorporating user feedback and optimizing your interaction with SAP Build. 

Background: 

To better understand user needs, we conducted extensive research involving interviews with a diverse group of users.  

A common theme in user feedback highlighted the need for a more intuitive project creation flow, starting with the user's objective before progressing to tool selection. Users expressed interest in new capabilities directly accessible from the Lobby, such as Node.js Full Stack and Java Full Stack options. 

Different Project Creation Options

Our findings guided us in redesigning the project creation process to align with user preferences and streamline the initial steps. 

What’s New? 

  • Intent-Based Navigation: The redesigned Project Creation experience prioritizes the user's goals and required tasks. Relevant project types are grouped logically to facilitate easier and faster selection, ensuring intuitive project management. 
  • Consistent Experience: In line with Fiori UX guidelines, we offer a seamless and consistent experience across the Project Creation Wizard and Extensibility Wizard. This step-by-step approach provides clarity and guidance, helping users make informed choices regarding their unique scenarios. 
  • Modern Look & Feel: Project creation options are categorized by Objectives, Categories, and Types, assisting developers in locating the right tools promptly. This organization allows for future expansion with additional capabilities. 

Next Steps: 

We value your input and invite you to share your experience with the new Project Creation Wizard. Your feedback is crucial for continued improvement. Use the "Share Product Feedback" option and click the "Give Feedback" button to let us know how this updated experience supports your professional objectives. 

Feedback

 

For more information, please visit SAP Help Documentation. 

We look forward to enhancing your project creation journey with SAP Build and ensuring our tools meet your business needs effectively. 

Already tried it? Please feel free to share your thoughts also in the comments below!

entry.title: Integrating AI into Business Processes with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) has rapidly become a crucial part of daily life, transforming how we interact with technology. From smart assistants to tailored recommendations, AI enhances convenience and personalization. Recognizing its potential, businesses are increasingly exploring AI integration to boost efficiency, creativity, and customer satisfaction. As companies aim to stay competitive, incorporating AI into traditional processes is becoming essential.

The aim of this article is to provide a practical guide for integrating AI services into traditional workflow processes, demonstrating how businesses can harness the power of AI to optimize operations and enhance decision-making. We will focus on two key technological components:

  • SAP Build Process Automation and
  • SAP Generative AI Hub.

SAP Build Process Automation offers a robust platform for automating and streamlining business processes, helping organizations to reduce manual tasks and increase efficiency. By utilizing this tool, businesses can create workflows that are not only faster but also adaptable to evolving needs.

Meanwhile, SAP Generative AI Hub provides access to cutting-edge AI capabilities that can be seamlessly integrated into these automated processes. This hub enables organizations to leverage AI-driven insights and functionalities, facilitating smarter decision-making and opening doors to innovative solutions.

Together, these tools create a powerful ecosystem where traditional workflows can be enriched with AI functionalities, driving transformative outcomes. In this article, we will explore step-by-step how to integrate these technologies into existing business processes, ensuring that businesses can capitalize on AI's full potential to lead in an increasingly competitive landscape.

A practical use case for integrating AI with SAP Build Process Automation involves managing overdue credits. Traditionally, drafting formal letters for debt settlement is time-consuming. By embedding SAP Generative AI Hub, businesses can automate the creation of personalized and accurate letters, enhancing efficiency and consistency. This AI-driven integration streamlines communication, boosting the likelihood of timely debt recovery and optimizing workflow, thus supporting proactive financial management.

The use case setup involved creating a SAP AI Core environment and using SAP AI Launchpad for deploying GPT-4o and the Orchestration service from the Generative AI Hub. The official documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/generative-ai-hub-in-sap-ai-core-7db524ee75e74bf8b50c167951fe34a5?locale=en-US) provides detailed instructions for completing the setup of the environment.

The solution's implementation starts with the creation of an optimized prompt to generate debt settlement letters using SAP Generative AI Hub. This prompt incorporates three key details: the client's name, the debt amount, and the language for the letter. By structuring these inputs effectively, the AI system can produce personalized, professional letters tailored to each debtor's case, enhancing efficiency and cross-linguistic communication in debt management.

For setting up the interaction workflow with the selected Large Language Model (LLM) using the Orchestration service, focus on these two key components:

  • Templating: This feature allows you to define and customize templates for interaction with the LLM. For your use case, you'll create templates that incorporate the three variables—client name, debt amount, and language preference. This ensures that the model processes the data correctly to generate the desired outputs.
  • Model Configuration: This involves setting up and configuring the specific LLM model you will use, such as GPT-4o. This includes selecting the model version, adjusting parameters for optimal performance, and managing model behavior to suit your specific requirements.

Templating ensures the information flows correctly into the system, while Model Configuration ensures the selected model operates efficiently and effectively to meet your needs.

To ensure the generated letter consistently meets specifications, we can employ the Few-Shot Prompting technique. This involves including one or more examples in the prompt to guide the LLM in understanding the desired response format. 

After creating the prompt, we can start testing it, running the Orchestration workflow and providing some sets of input variables. In particular, we can check the ability to generate the letter in multiple languages.

Once the workflow has achieved the desired level of accuracy, we can proceed to expose it as a REST API. This allows external applications and services to interact with your workflow seamlessly. By exposing our workflow as an API, we enable integration with other systems, facilitate automation, and enhance accessibility for users needing to generate debt settlement letters programmatically, thereby streamlining the entire process.

At this point, it's important to copy the JSON description of the Orchestration workflow, because it will be used to invoke the API:

To expose our workflow as a REST API, we first need to identify the endpoint where the service will be made available. This can be done by accessing the AI Launchpad and navigating to the deployment section of the Orchestration service. There, we will find details regarding the endpoint configuration.

The API authentication requirements should be defined by the SAP Business Technology Platform subaccount administrator. For testing the API from an external location, we need to provide the Authentication Url, the Client ID and the Client Secret. When using the API from a BTP subaccount, we just need to define from the BTP Cockpit a Destination and then the application running in that subaccount will be able to use the API without providing the credentials.

To test the REST API, we can use Bruno, an application available on multiple platforms that facilitates the generation of REST calls and the visualization of results. Bruno is a useful tool for debugging and validating our API, as it allows us to:

  • Create and send various types of HTTP requests to your API endpoint.
  • View and analyze the responses, including headers and body content.
  • Identify and troubleshoot any error messages or issues in the API communication.

Using Bruno, we can ensure that our API is functioning correctly and delivering the expected outcomes before integrating it into live environments.

To configure Bruno for testing your REST API, we need to create a Collection and set up an Environment with a series of variables. Here's how we can do it:

1. Create a Collection:

  • Open Bruno and navigate to the option for creating a new Collection.
  • Name our Collection appropriately based on the API we're testing.
  • Inside this Collection, we can organize different API requests related to our workflow.

2. Set up an Environment:

  • Create a new Environment within Bruno where we can define variables that are used in our requests.
  • Include essential variables such as:
    • `deploymentUrl`: The endpoint URL of your API.
    • `Auth_Url`: The URL for the Authentication.
    • `Client_ID` and `Secret` for the Authentication
    • `Content-Type`: the type of the content of the request

3. Configure Requests:

  • Use these variables in your requests by referencing them in the URL, headers, or body.
  • This approach allows us to easily switch configurations if needed and run tests under different conditions.

In our case, we need to configure a POST requests that sends a message to the Orchestration workflow endpoint. The header of the request should contain the Content Type and the Resource Group where the Orchestration workflow is running in the AI Core service:

The body of the request should contain the description of the worklow process, in JSON format. Also in this case it is possible to define variables containing the Customer name, the Amount and Language of the letter, for easily testing multiple combinations.

   

After assigning some values to the variables, we can run the request:

Now it's important to carefully browse the response, to identify in the JSON structure the property where the content of the letter is provided:

module_result.llm.choices[0].message.content

The last step of our activity consists in the integration of the text generation functionality of our API into the SAP Build Process Automation process that manages the unpaid debt collection. Here an Automation, named NotificationCreate, has been implemented that attaches to the context of the workflow the PDF file of the generated letter. 

If you're interested in learning how to automatically attach a generated document into a SAP Build Process Automation workflow, you can read my previous blog article: SAP Build Process Automation: using workflow attachments with robotic automations 

The NotificationCreate automation includes another automation (GenerateLetter) that manages the API call:

This automation receives as input parameters the three variables we defined at the beginning of our implementation: the name of the customer, the amount of the debt and the language of the letter and it includes few simple steps:

Create Output Variable: an empty variable (letterText) is defined for collecting the final result.

Create Content: the prompt is generated and saved in a text variable, named content. In creation of the prompt, we're embedding the three variables we received as input parameters.

Custom Script: a script that prepares the options to be used when invoking the API. The invocation of the API should use the same configuration we tested before with Bruno:

let jsonText = JSON.stringify( { 
   orchestration_config: { 
    module_configurations: {
      llm_module_config: {
        model_name: "gpt-4o",
        model_params: {},
        model_version: "2024-08-06"
      },
      templating_module_config: {
        template: [ 
          {
            role: "user", 
            content: content
          } 
        ],
        defaults: {} 
      } 
        }
      }
} );


let options = {
    url: '/completion' ,
    method: 'POST',
    responseType: 'json',
    resolveBodyOnly: true ,
    body: jsonText,
    headers: {
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
            'AI-Resource-Group': 'default'
    }		
};

return options;

Then we enter in a Try/Catch branch, that allow us to capture and debug the API related errors.

Call Web Service with Destination: executes to API call, using the options created by the Custom Script. Please notice that the endpoint of the API call here is provided as a BTP Destination. The security is managed by the BTP and the programmer does not need to deal with the authorization credentials, because they are centrally managed by the administrators.

Set Variable Value: the response from the API call is parsed and the text of the generated letter is extracted and saved into the letterText variable. This variable will be used as final ouput of the automation.

Now the implementation of the integration with the Orchestration service is complete. The text of the letter has been generated by the AI service and it can be used to create a PDF document ready to be sent to the customer.

In this article, we've explored the integration of Generative AI services into traditional workflows using an API-centric approach. This method not only enhances process automation but also opens up possibilities for business innovation by extending existing systems with the powerful capabilities of Generative AI.

By exposing  REST APIs, we've demonstrated how Generative AI services, such as automatic text generation, can be directly integrated into business workflows. This approach allows leveraging AI's power and flexibility without completely redesigning existing systems.

Moreover, API integration is highly versatile, applicable to a wide range of applications beyond traditional automation processes. Whether CRM, ERP, or other enterprise software, APIs facilitate easy access to advanced AI functionalities, promoting adaptation and scalability of IT solutions.

APIs act as bridges between Generative AI innovation and established solutions, ensuring a seamless transition and boosting digital transformation. The examples and approaches discussed in this blog equip developers and IT professionals with practical guidance to expand automation boundaries, aiming for greater efficiency, insights, and added value in their organizations.

And that’s all for today! Now you can continue to follow the SAP AI Core Topic page: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/ai-core-artificial-intelligence, read other posts on the SAP Community Blogs: https://community.sap.com/t5/all-sap-community-blogs/ct-p/all-blogs or become a Certified SAP Generative AI Developer: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/https-ondemand-questionmark-eu-delivery-open-php-session-0449315000449315-customerid-602316-name-sample-group-ts#how-to-prepare.

entry.title: Introducing the New Pricing and Packaging for SAP Build: A Unified Experience for all Developers
entry.content:
As SAP is evolving its Business Suite, AI, and Data Products to meet rising market demands, and as part of this strategy, we are introducing a new exciting, unified commercial model for SAP Build. This updated offering consolidates capabilities for pro-code, low code and AI powered development tools making it easier than ever to build applications and extensions within the SAP ecosystem.

With built-in access to runtime, automation, and extensibility services, SAP Build now empowers both business users and professional developers to choose the right tool set for the right job. The integration of generative AI enhances productivity by streamlining workflows, automating tasks, and accelerating decision-making.

To further support development, SAP Build capabilities along with SAP HANA Cloud are now fully included in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Packages empowering customers to handle the majority of their expected scenarios right out of the box. Read the full announcement here.

What changes?

We've heard our customers loud and clear; they asked for simpler licensing, easier provisioning, and a unified toolset. Previously, each SAP Build product like Build Apps, Process Automation, Work Zone, Build Code, and ABAP was licensed separately. This resulted in having lots of different services for customers to manage.  

The following list of 14 services from SAP Build have now been consolidated:

SAP Build Services:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Mobile Services
  • Application Frontend Service
  • SAP Business Application Studio

 SAP Build Runtime Services:

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  • SAP Document Management Service
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management
  • SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery

 Now you can access the full range of SAP Build capabilities listed above through just four distinct licenses:

SAP Build base user

This edition is for users who need to access applications, gain insights for day-to-day business needs, and approve tasks. SAP Build base user includes:

  • Access to digital workspaces
  • Access web and mobile apps
  • Request and approve workflow tasks
  • Access mobile start
  • 1000 API Calls
  • 100 MB Storage

 Note: Prerequisite is at least one Build Developer license to manage SAP Build.

 SAP Build premium user

This edition is for users who author content, monitor processes, customize pages, and deploy bots. SAP Build premium user includes:

  • Everything in SAP Build base user, plus
  • Monitor processes via the dashboard
  • Deploy attended robotic process automation bots
  • Author and access workspace content
  • Visually design digital workspaces and guided experiences
  • 1000 API Calls
  • 100 MB Storage

 Note: Prerequisite is at least one Build Developer license to manage SAP Build.

SAP Build developer

This edition is for users who develop application/extensions, workflows, and automations for S/4 and other LoB apps. SAP Build developer includes:

  • Everything in SAP Build premium user, plus
  • Build with low-code/no-code developer tools
  • Develop with code-first developer tools (IDEs)
  • Create and deploy apps and extensions
  • Develop and deploy mobile applications
  • Design and deploy workflows
  • 5000 API Calls
  • 500 MB Storage

SAP Build runtime

Required for application-hosting and lifecycle management services that support the creation and execution for dev/test as well as production environments. SAP Build runtime includes:

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime
  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  • Application runtime
  • Document management storage and API
  • Cloud transport management
  • Continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)

Note: Customers can access applications hosted on runtimes without using any end-user capabilities. This ensures customers aren't charged for user licenses they don’t actively use.

Find detailed information about SAP Build and what’s in scope on the SAP Help Portal. 

What are the key benefits of the new SAP Build packaging, and how will it help our customers?

The new SAP Build packaging delivers a streamlined, powerful experience for building extensions, automations, and apps across the SAP ecosystem. Here's how it benefits customers:

  • Unified Access Across Tools: SAP Build now brings together low-code, pro-code, and AI-powered development tools under a single license removing the complexity of managing multiple products and licenses.
  • Built-In ERP Integration: SAP Build and SAP HANA Cloud entitlements are now included in SAP Cloud ERP Private Packages, enabling faster innovation and development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers.
  • Empowerment for Developers & Business Users: Both professional developers and key users gain the flexibility to personalize SAP systems with greater speed up to 3x faster, according to
  • Optimized Cost & Efficiency: A flexible, usage-based runtime model helps reduce total cost of ownership and streamline operational overhead.
  • Simplified Governance: A unified licensing approach improves compliance management and centralizes governance across all tools.
  • Business Value First: By removing commercial and technical barriers, organizations can focus more on driving transformation and delivering real business outcomes.

With SAP Build, customers can:

  • Scale confidently within the SAP ecosystem: Use included licenses to rapidly build compliant extensions that align with SAP standards and scale across the enterprise.
  • Leverage AI to boost productivity: Automate complex cross-system processes with AI agents, customize Joule with skill-specific tasks, and speed up development using SAP-optimized AI tools.
  • Deliver faster from front-end to full-stack: Build complete extensions with prebuilt templates, low-code tools, and integrated services like authentication, deployment, and lifecycle management—all on a single, seamless platform.

 Commercial Model and Availability

As of 22nd April 2025, SAP Build is available to customers for subscription only. Availability for consumption under BTPEA and PayGo is anticipated by the end of Q2.

Call to action

  • Visit the SAP Build pricing page to learn more
  • Help portal documentation
  • Register for the SAP Build webinar on May 8th
  • Read the full announcement here
  • Visit the SAP Discovery Center for more details.
  • Review pricing on Discovery Center

entry.title: Partners unlock innovation with latest SAP Build capabilities
entry.content:
20 Partners from 10 countries participated and built innovative solutions during the recently concluded Hack2Build on SAP Build. With the latest SAP Build capabilities delivered recently by SAP, Partners explored and leveraged key functionalities of the low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools for building their innovations.

Partners extend, create, and automate with SAP Build

In this Hack2Build edition, partner used SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Process Automation integration and built applications to guide users through a multi-step process and efficient completion of complex business processes. Partners also used SAP Build Code, the AI-powered code assistance for application creation and code optimization.
